quality.rationale: 多步微分链式法则结合洛伦兹速度变换与矢量三重积，逻辑严密但属纯理论运动学推演

origin.question:
[文件 1: 1_s1_1.16.md]

Problem 1.16. A particle moves with 3-velocity $\underline{\mathbf{u}}$ and 3-acceleration $\underline{\mathbf{a}}$ as seen by an inertial observer $\mathcal{O}$ . Another inertial observer $\mathcal{O}'$ has 3-velocity $\underline{\mathbf{v}}$ relative to $\mathcal{O}$ . Show that the components of acceleration of the particle parallel and perpendicular to $\underline{\mathbf{v}}$ as measured by $\mathcal{O}'$ are
$$
\begin{array}{l}
\underline {{\mathsf {a}}}^{\prime} \| = \frac {(1 - \mathrm {v}^{2})^{3 / 2}}{(1 - \underline {{\mathrm {v}}} \cdot \underline {{\mathrm {u}}})^{3}} \underline {{\mathsf {a}}} \|
\\
\underline {{{\mathsf {a}}}}_{\perp}^{\prime} = \frac {(1 - \mathsf {v}^{2})}{(1 - \underline {{{\mathsf {v}}}} \cdot \underline {{{\mathsf {u}}}})^{3}} \left[ \underline {{{\mathsf {a}}}}_{\perp} - \underline {{{\mathsf {v}}}} \times (\underline {{{\mathsf {a}}}} \times \underline {{{\mathsf {u}}}}) \right].
\\
\end{array}
$$
origin.solution:
## Solution

Solution 1.16. The transformation of 3-velocity, from Problem 1.8, is
$$
u_{\perp}^{\prime} = \frac {\gamma^{- 1}}{(1 - v u_{\parallel})} u_{\perp}, \quad u_{\parallel} = \frac {u_{\parallel} - v}{1 - v u_{\parallel}}
$$
and the time transformation is
$$
t^{\prime} = \gamma (t - \underline {v} \cdot \underline {x}).
$$
Now take differentials:
$$
\mathrm {d} u_{\perp}^{\prime} = \frac {\gamma^{- 1} \mathrm {d} u_{\perp}}{(1 - \mathrm {v} u_{\parallel})} + \frac {\mathrm {v} \gamma^{- 1} u_{\perp}}{(1 - \mathrm {v} u_{\parallel})^{2}} \mathrm {d} u_{\parallel} = \frac {\gamma^{- 1} [ (1 - \mathrm {v} u_{\parallel}) \mathrm {d} u_{\perp} + \mathrm {v} u_{\perp} \mathrm {d} u_{\parallel} ]}{(1 - \mathrm {v} u_{\parallel})^{2}}
$$
$$
\mathrm {d} u_{\parallel}^{\prime} = \frac {\mathrm {d} u_{\parallel}}{1 - v u_{\parallel}} - \frac {v (v - u_{\parallel}) d u_{\parallel}}{(1 - v u_{\parallel})^{2}} = \frac {\gamma^{- 2} d u_{\parallel}}{(1 - v u_{\parallel})^{2}}
$$
$$
\mathrm {d t}^{\prime} = \gamma (\mathrm {d t} - \underline {v} \cdot \underline {d x}) = \gamma \mathrm {d t} (1 - \underline {v} \cdot \underline {u}) = \gamma \mathrm {d t} (1 - v u_{\parallel})
$$
$$
\frac {\mathrm {d} \underline {u}^{\prime}}{\mathrm {d} t^{\prime}} \equiv \underline {a}^{\prime} \| = \frac {\gamma^{- 3}}{(1 - \mathrm {v} u_{\|})^{3}} \underline {a} \| = \frac {\gamma^{- 3}}{(1 - \underline {v} \cdot \underline {u})^{3}} \underline {a} \|. \quad (\text {A n s w e r})
$$
and
$$
\begin{array}{l}
\frac {\mathrm {d} \underline {u}_{\perp}^{\prime}}{\mathrm {d} t^{\prime}} \equiv \underline {a}_{\perp}^{\prime} = \frac {\gamma^{- 2}}{(1 - \mathrm {v u}_{\parallel})^{3}} \left[ (1 - \mathrm {v u}_{\parallel}) \underline {a}_{\perp} + \mathrm {v} \underline {u}_{\perp} a_{\parallel} \right] \\
= \frac {\gamma^{- 2}}{(1 - v u_{\|})^{3}} [ \underline {a}_{\perp} - v (u_{\|} \underline {a}_{\perp} - \underline {u}_{\perp} a_{\|}) ] \\
= \frac {\gamma^{- 2}}{(1 - \underline {\mathrm {v}} \cdot \underline {\mathrm {u}})^{3}} [ \underline {\mathrm {a}}_{\perp} - \underline {\mathrm {v}} \times (\underline {\mathrm {a}} \times \underline {\mathrm {u}}) ]. \tag {Answer} \\
\end{array}
$$
In the last line we have used the fact that
$$
\begin{array}{l}
\underline {{\underline {a}}} \times \underline {{\underline {u}}} = (\underline {{a}}_{\perp} + \underline {{a}}_{\parallel}) \times (\underline {{u}}_{\perp} + \underline {{u}}_{\parallel}) \\
= \underline {\mathbf {a}}_{\perp} \times \underline {\mathbf {u}} \| + \underline {\mathbf {a}} \| \times \underline {\mathbf {u}}_{\perp} \\
\end{array}
$$
and the rule for expanding vector triple products.]
